content:
type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: I'll own types.py since our changes both touch ImageBlock's mimetype/base64 logic and are likely to conflict. Please send a SPEC for get_image_mimetype() so I can implement it alongside normalize_image_bytes().

fields.note: I'll own types.py since our changes both touch ImageBlock's mimetype/base64 logic and are likely to conflict. Please send a SPEC for get_image_mimetype() so I can implement it alongside normalize_image_bytes().

content:
type: DEFER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
spec: Add a module-level function normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes] in types.py (near the top, after imports, before ImageBlock class). Behavior: Given raw image bytes that MAY already be base64-encoded, determine whether the input is already base64-encoded. Try base64.b64decode(image, validate=True); if it succeeds without raising, treat input as already-encoded: return (image, decoded_bytes) where decoded_bytes is the b64-decoded raw bytes (for mimetype guessing). If b64decode raises (binascii.Error/ValueError) or otherwise indicates invalid base64, treat input as raw bytes: return (base64.b64encode(image), image) i.e. (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) — encoded_image is always base64-encoded bytes, decoded_for_guessing is always raw (non-base64) bytes suitable for filetype.guess(). Then refactor ImageBlock.image_to_base64 (the @model_validator(mode='after') method) to: if not self.image: return self; else call encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image); if not self.image_mimetype: guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing); self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None; then self.image = encoded_image; return self. Need 'import binascii' added if used for exception handling. This preserves existing behavior for raw-bytes input (encodes + guesses mimetype from raw bytes) while adding support for already-base64-encoded input (avoids double-encoding, still guesses mimetype from decoded bytes).
note: normalize_image_bytes + image_to_base64 refactor
